title: Commentary on Policy/Limits Compliance: May 31, 2026
content:
The credit union's compliance picture as of May 31, 2026 is largely compliant overall but facing notable compliance gaps in several areas. Profitability metrics remain within targets and limits; however, liquidity ratios are significantly below their benchmarks across the board, growth metrics show negative performance against positive targets, interest rate margins fall short of best practice thresholds, capital growth lags behind its annual target despite strong solvency positions, and asset quality is breached by delinquency levels exceeding policy.

Asset Quality
Classified assets as a percentage of net worth are at -0.78%, well within the < 40% limit. Net charge-offs averaged 0.50% against average loans, staying comfortably below the < 0.75% threshold. However, delinquent loans to total loans reached 1.73%, which exceeds the established policy limit of less than 1.5%. This single breach in asset quality represents a material concern requiring management attention.

Capital & Solvency
The net worth ratio stands at 24.67% against a benchmark of ≥ 10%, indicating robust capitalization relative to total assets. The solvency evaluation is recorded at an impressive 133.33%, far exceeding the minimum requirement of 7%. Net worth growth for the period was 4.12%, which falls short of its stated target of ≥ 5% year-over-year.

Profitability & Growth
Net margin on average assets achieved 2.95%, comfortably above the ≥ 1.00% best practice floor, while net operating expenses averaged 1.91% against a ≤ 3.00% limit and return on average assets reached 0.95% versus its ≥ 0.80% target—all within acceptable ranges. However, loan growth contracted at -27.52%, membership growth declined by -1.95%, and market share shrank by -11.03%; all three metrics are below their respective positive targets of 5%-10%, ≥ Market Avg (for shares), and 3%-7%.

Interest Rate & Funding
Cost of funds averaged 1.41%, remaining within the ≤ 1.50% limit, while yield on average loans stood at a strong 8.25% against its ≥ 4.00% benchmark. However, net interest margin per asset was only 2.85%, which is below the best practice target of ≥ 3.00%. Additionally, cash and short-term investments represented just 5.66% of total assets versus a required range of 8%-15%, regular shares to total funding were at 57.70% against a minimum of 70%, and loans as a percentage of both total assets (31.28%) and total shares (42.25%) are substantially below their respective targets.

The credit union maintains strong capital buffers and profitable operations, but faces genuine concerns around liquidity adequacy, negative growth trajectories across key market metrics, and elevated delinquency levels that breach policy limits. Management will prioritize addressing the shortfall in net worth growth to meet its 5% annual target while implementing strategies to improve loan portfolio composition and reduce delinquent exposure.
